agent_result: The title of the first paper authored by the one that had authored prior papers is "A New Software Agent 'Learning' Algorithm".
question: Of the authors (First M. Last) that worked on the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" in 2015, what was the title of the first paper authored by the one that had authored prior papers?
full_query: Of the authors (First M. Last) that worked on the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" in 2015, what was the title of the first paper authored by the one that had authored prior papers?
golden_answer: Mapping Human Oriented Information to Software Agents for Online Systems Usage

agent_trajectory.name: plan
agent_trajectory.value:
## Goal 1: Determine the first paper title using bibliographic database search
- Path 1.1: DBLP search for the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" (2015) → retrieve author list → for each author, access their DBLP profile → sort publications by year → identify the author(s) with any publication year <2015 → select the earliest publication title for that author.
  - Success: Obtain a valid title of the earliest paper (year <2015) for the author who had prior publications.
- Path 1.2: ACM Digital Library search for the same paper → extract author names → for each author, search ACM DL author page → find their earliest published work → verify year <2015 → output that first paper title.
  - Success: Same as above – a correct first-paper title for the qualified author.

## Goal 2: Determine the first paper title using general web search and author profiling
- Path 2.1: Google search query: “Pie Menus or Linear Menus 2015” “first paper” “author prior” → crawl the most relevant result (e.g., a blog, news article, or research summary) that may directly mention both the 2015 paper and the author’s first paper.
  - Success: Extract a clear, complete title of the first paper.
- Path 2.2: Google Scholar search for the 2015 paper → click on each author’s profile → navigate to “All publications” sorted by year → locate the earliest publication (year <2015) → record its title.
  - Success: A verifiable first paper title from an official scholar profile.

content.text:
New task:
Of the authors (First M. Last) that worked on the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" in 2015, what was the title of the first paper authored by the one that had authored prior papers?

————Memory System Guidance————
[Context Note] For reference: similar tasks have execution for: what was the volume in m^3 of the fish bag that was calculated in the university of leicester paper
————End Memory————
Now, begin your planning analysis for this task!

[PLAN]:
## Goal 1: Determine the first paper title using bibliographic database search
- Path 1.1: DBLP search for the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" (2015) → retrieve author list → for each author, access their DBLP profile → sort publications by year → identify the author(s) with any publication year <2015 → select the earliest publication title for that author.
  - Success: Obtain a valid title of the earliest paper (year <2015) for the author who had prior publications.
- Path 1.2: ACM Digital Library search for the same paper → extract author names → for each author, search ACM DL author page → find their earliest published work → verify year <2015 → output that first paper title.
  - Success: Same as above – a correct first-paper title for the qualified author.

## Goal 2: Determine the first paper title using general web search and author profiling
- Path 2.1: Google search query: “Pie Menus or Linear Menus 2015” “first paper” “author prior” → crawl the most relevant result (e.g., a blog, news article, or research summary) that may directly mention both the 2015 paper and the author’s first paper.
  - Success: Extract a clear, complete title of the first paper.
- Path 2.2: Google Scholar search for the 2015 paper → click on each author’s profile → navigate to “All publications” sorted by year → locate the earliest publication (year <2015) → record its title.
  - Success: A verifiable first paper title from an official scholar profile.

————Memory System Guidance————
[Past Experience (for reference)] For reference: similar tasks have execution for: what was the volume in m^3 of the fish bag that was calculated in the university of leicester paper
————End Memory————
